@graph.description: RunStrict builds location-based GPS running games that turn daily runs into team-based territorial warfare.

@graph.name: RunStrict

@graph.name: RunStrict
@graph.description: A free location-based GPS running game where players capture hexagonal territory tiles by physically running through them. Choose from three teams — FLAME, WAVE, or CHAOS — and compete to dominate your city before the 40-day season resets in The Void. Uses Uber's H3 hex grid at resolution 9 (~175m tiles). Anti-cheat verified with accelerometer and GPS accuracy checks.
featureList: GPS territory capture on H3 hex grid (~175m tiles)
40-day competitive seasons with full reset (The Void)
Anti-cheat: accelerometer verification, GPS accuracy ≤50m, 25 km/h speed cap
Privacy-first: no runner IDs stored in hex data
Apple Watch companion (watchOS 10+) and Wear OS companion (Wear OS 3+, Galaxy Watch 4+ / Pixel Watch) for live pace and hex capture alerts on the wrist

@graph.name: How to Play RunStrict — GPS Territory Capture Running Game
@graph.description: Learn how to capture hexagonal territory tiles by running in the RunStrict GPS running game. Four simple steps from lacing up to earning buff multipliers.

step.name: Start Running with GPS Tracking
step.text: Open RunStrict and start running. GPS tracks your route in real-time. Maintain under 8 min/km pace to activate hex capture mode. Anti-cheat verifies physical movement via accelerometer.

step.name: Flip Hexagonal Territory Tiles
step.text: Every ~175m hexagonal tile (Uber H3 resolution 9) you physically run through flips to your team's color — FLAME (Red), WAVE (Blue), or CHAOS (Purple). The last runner through a hex controls it.

step.name: Dominate the City Map
step.text: Watch your city transform as you claim territory. Earn points for every hex captured, climb Zone, District, and Province leaderboards, and lead your team to territorial dominance.

step.name: Earn Buff Multipliers
step.text: FLAME Elites (top 20% of their province) reach up to 5x point multipliers. WAVE members rise together up to 4x through district and province wins plus team turnout. CHAOS power scales with province participation up to 4x. An honest 1x floor means a no-bonus run scores exactly 1x. Multipliers reset each 40-day season.

@graph.name: RunStrict Teams Comparison
@graph.description: Compare the three teams in RunStrict: FLAME (Red), WAVE (Blue), and CHAOS (Purple). Each team has unique buff mechanics and play styles.

itemListElement.name: FLAME (Red Team)
itemListElement.description: Performance-based meritocracy. The top 20% of each province's Red runners become Elites, reaching up to 5x points. Common runners earn 1x-3x from district and province wins (honest 1x floor). Best for competitive solo runners.

itemListElement.name: WAVE (Blue Team)
itemListElement.description: Collective strength. Every Blue runner rises together: a district win, a province win, and team turnout (50%+ of your province running) each add a point, up to 4x shared equally. Best for team-oriented runners.

itemListElement.name: CHAOS (Purple Team)
itemListElement.description: The wildcard. Join anytime but membership is irreversible. Power scales with province participation: 1x base, 2x at 25% active, 3x at 50%, 4x at 75%. No territory bonuses. Best for social runners who thrive under pressure.

acceptedAnswer.text: RunStrict is a free location-based GPS running game where your physical runs capture hexagonal territory tiles on a real map of your city. Choose from three teams — FLAME, WAVE, or CHAOS — and compete to control your city before the 40-day season ends in The Void reset. It uses Uber's H3 hex grid system with ~175m tiles.

mainEntity.name: How does hex capture work in RunStrict?

acceptedAnswer.text: As you run, RunStrict uses GPS to track your route in real-time. Every ~175m hexagonal tile (H3 resolution 9) you physically run through flips to your team's color. The last runner through a hex controls it. Maintain under 8 min/km pace to activate capture mode. Anti-cheat uses accelerometer verification and GPS accuracy checks.
Yes, RunStrict is completely free to download and play. Available now on iOS (App Store) and Android (Google Play).

mainEntity.name: What is The Void in RunStrict?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Void is the season reset event that occurs every 40 days. When The Void arrives, all captured hexes, points, and territorial control are completely wiped. Only the season leaderboard legend survives. Then a new 40-day season begins.

mainEntity.name: What teams can I join in RunStrict?

acceptedAnswer.text: RunStrict has three teams: FLAME (Red — top 20% of each province become Elites, up to 5× through performance), WAVE (Blue — collective bonuses up to 4× when your district or province wins and turnout is high), and CHAOS (Purple — join anytime, irreversible, a participation ladder up to 4×).

mainEntity.name: Is RunStrict available on iPhone and Android?

acceptedAnswer.text: RunStrict is available now on iOS (App Store, requires iOS 15+) and Android (Google Play, requires Android 10+), with brand-new Apple Watch (watchOS 10+) and Wear OS (Wear OS 3+, Galaxy Watch 4+ / Pixel Watch) companion apps. GPS accuracy of 50m or better is required for hex capture.

mainEntity.name: How is RunStrict different from Strava or Nike Run Club?

acceptedAnswer.text: Unlike Strava or Nike Run Club which focus on personal fitness tracking, RunStrict adds a competitive territory-capture game layer to running. Every run is a strategic move in a team-based territorial war — you flip hex tiles, earn buff multipliers, and compete on leaderboards. It combines the physical activity of a running app with the strategy of a location-based game.

mainEntity.name: How is RunStrict different from Pokemon GO?

acceptedAnswer.text: RunStrict requires actual running with pace verification (under 8 min/km), not just walking. It focuses on real athletic performance with anti-cheat measures including accelerometer verification and GPS accuracy checks. Territory is earned through physical running effort, not catching virtual creatures.

mainEntity.name: Does RunStrict drain my phone battery?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. RunStrict uses battery-optimized 0.5 Hz GPS polling (once every 2 seconds) and syncs data only on app launch, resume, and run completion. There are no persistent WebSocket connections draining your battery.

mainEntity.name: Can you cheat in RunStrict with GPS spoofing?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. RunStrict uses multi-layer anti-cheat: 25 km/h speed cap, accelerometer verification to confirm physical movement, GPS accuracy must be ≤50m, and runners must maintain under 8 min/km pace for hex capture to activate.
RunStrict works globally in any city with GPS coverage. The hex grid covers the entire planet. Available on iOS (App Store) and Android (Google Play) worldwide.
